facade_colour: dark charcoal and black facade panels with green-lit retail glazing and bright interior illumination at grade

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: dark charcoal and black facade panels with green-lit retail glazing and bright interior illumination at grade
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Mid-rise mixed-use building appears recent and well maintained, with active retail at street level and occupied upper residential floors.
photo_observations.window_details: Upper floors use a disciplined grid of rectangular windows and darker spandrel/panel sections; retail level is more transparent.
photo_observations.door_details: Main retail entrance and residential/commercial secondary entrance are visible under bright glazed bays.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple modern flat roofline with slight stepback at upper levels.
photo_observations.porch_details: No porch; building sits directly on the sidewalk.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Modern composition relies on contrasting dark panels, vertical signage, and illuminated retail frontage.
photo_observations.storefront_description: A wide, brightly glazed retail base anchors the building with T&T Supermarket branding, a prominent main shopping entrance, and a separate secondary entry integrated into the dark modern facade.
photo_observations.signage_observed: T&T Supermarket and building number signage are visible.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Appears to be a recent infill or comprehensive redevelopment rather than a heavily altered older building.
photo_observations.notes: Best seen in IMG_20260321_013321378_HDR.jpg and IMG_20260321_013327971_HDR.jpg.

facade_detail.composition: The street frontage spans roughly 6.1 metres across 7 storeys beneath a flat roof with upper stepback, consistent with its commercial typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The visible opening pattern is organized as 3, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Key storefront and facade features include cornice.

facade_description: 297 College St (T&T Supermarket + others) presents a commercial street frontage within the Kensington Market HCD. The street frontage spans roughly 6.1 metres across 7 storeys beneath a flat roof with upper stepback, consistent with its commercial typology. The frontage is defined primarily by mixed in a dark charcoal and black facade panels with green-lit retail glazing and bright interior illumination at grade palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 3, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances. Key storefront and facade features include cornice.
